stem: When nothing is being asked of me...
a: I drift toward what helps me feel settled
b: I drift toward what I want to understand better

signal.construct: inquiry-seeking vs settling-seeking in free inner space
signal.pole_a: attention moves toward ease, comfort, and low-demand settling
signal.pole_b: attention moves toward understanding, questions, and unfinished threads
signal.note: Use as a weak prior only. This is mainly a 5-vs-9 style sorter, not a full head-vs-gut classifier.

stem: At the end of a great day...
a: What stays with me is that I mattered to someone
b: What stays with me is that I rose to the task

signal.construct: relational significance vs performance/challenge as the day's emotional anchor
signal.pole_a: value lands in having mattered to another person
signal.pole_b: value lands in having met challenge or performed well
signal.note: Use as a weak prior only. This is about where self-value lands after a good day, not simply helping vs achieving.

stem: When I get hurt in a close relationship...
a: I want time alone before I let them near it
b: I want a response from them sooner rather than later

signal.construct: contact-seeking vs private containment after relational hurt
signal.pole_a: hurt is contained first and opened later, if at all
signal.pole_b: hurt increases the wish for response, contact, or repair
signal.note: Use as a weak prior only. This tests what hurt creates first: a pull toward contact or a pull toward privacy.

stem: When something feels off in a close relationship...
a: I wait until I know exactly what I mean
b: I bring it up while the heat is still there

signal.construct: entering conflict during activation vs after private clarification
signal.pole_b: moves into repair or confrontation while the issue is still hot
signal.note: Use as a weak prior only. This is about timing of engagement, not maturity, courage, or communication skill.

stem: When tension rises between people I care about...
a: I ease back and try not to get pulled into it
b: I move in and try to shape what happens next

signal.construct: entering relational tension vs reducing involvement
signal.pole_a: reduces involvement to preserve inner steadiness
signal.pole_b: engages tension to influence, steer, or stabilize it
signal.note: Use as a weak prior only. This is about stance toward interpersonal tension, not general boldness or introversion.

stem: When someone crosses a line with me...
a: I understand I was angry after the moment passes
b: I feel the anger right away before I think

signal.construct: immediate vs delayed access to anger
signal.pole_a: anger is recognized later after distance or reinterpretation
signal.pole_b: anger is conscious quickly and felt in the body
signal.note: Use as a weak prior only. This is mainly about access to anger, not direct type assignment.
